Title: Biosynthesis of very Long-chain fatty acids is required for Arabidopsis auxin-mediated embryonic and post-embryonic development

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (SDR) superfamily; Rossmann fold; catalytic Ser-Tyr-Lys triad; ER fatty acid elongase component
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential structural components of sphingolipids and other lipids; required for proper endomembrane trafficking, PIN polarity, auxin distribution, and embryonic/post-embryonic development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and shoots; enriched in membrane sphingolipids
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ER fatty acid elongase complex / VLCFA elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates during each elongation cycle of very-long-chain fatty acid biosynthesis using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms elongation intermediate required for VLCFA production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes VLCFA accumulation; the viable kcr1-2 allele reduces fatty acyl elongation and lowers VLCFA and VLCFA-containing sphingolipid levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. esm was mapped to KCR1 (At1g67730), and pKCR1::KCR1-GFP fully complemented the mutant. 2. The kcr1-2 missense allele (Gly184Ser) reduced major VLCFAs in roots and shoots and altered sphingolipid acyl-chain composition. 3. Reduced KCR1 function caused defects in PIN trafficking/polarity, disturbed auxin distribution, and severe embryonic, root, lateral root, and shoot meristem phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed with enrichment in lateral root cap, endodermis, boundaries of lateral root primordia and emerged lateral roots, trichome basal cells, stomatal guard cells; in embryos first detected at ~32-cell stage in protoderm and later in a belt-like pattern below the SAM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant mapping; allelism test with kcr1-1; genetic complementation with pKCR1::KCR1-GFP; FAME profiling by GC-MS; sphingolipid profiling by UPLC-ESI-MS/MS; localization of KCR1-GFP
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin analog NAA; Brefeldin A; FM4-64 endocytic tracer; NaCl and sorbitol osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe developmental defects, reduced root growth, malformed cotyledons, altered lateral root emergence, adult dwarfing/leaf curling, hypersensitivity to excess water and osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The studied viable allele is kcr1-2 (G888A; Gly184Ser), whereas kcr1-1 is embryo lethal. The paper links KCR1-derived VLCFAs to membrane sphingolipid homeostasis and specific effects on PIN trafficking rather than broad loss of polarity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: KCR1 is a critical control point for VLCFA homeostasis, but partial loss causes major developmental penalties; engineering should avoid strong knockdown and instead use tightly controlled or tissue-specific modulation if manipulating VLCFA-related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Next-generation sequencing for mutant mapping; data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pKCR1::KCR1-GFP (native promoter, endogenous/context-specific); pPIN1::PIN1-GFP; pPIN2::PIN2-GFP; DR5rev::GFP; p35S::RFP-HDEL
